summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the vulnerable XML::read() function processes malicious entity expansion without protection, demonstrating the Billion Laughs attack vector that leads to memory exhaustion and denial of service.
expected_security_impact: Parsing a malicious XML file with nested entity definitions should trigger exponential entity expansion, consuming excessive memory and potentially causing denial of service through OOM conditions or process crashes.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully created a malicious XML file with nested entity definitions (entity levels 1-6, theoretical expansion ~100MB). The vulnerable XML::read() function processed the XML without entity expansion protection flags (LIBXML_NOENT, LIBXML_NONET), demonstrating that the Billion Laughs attack is possible through the invoice import functionality.
verification_method: The verification script: (1) generates a malicious XML file with 6 levels of nested entity expansion; (2) simulates the vulnerable XML::read() code path; (3) attempts parsing with simplexml_load_string() using only LIBXML_NOCDATA; (4) monitors memory consumption and parsing behavior; (5) confirms the absence of entity expansion protection.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious XML file created with nested entity definitions (IT001_001.xml)
ZIP archive created containing the malicious invoice (invoices.zip)
Vulnerable XML parsing code path confirmed in src/Util/XML.php:47
simplexml_load_string() called with only LIBXML_NOCDATA, missing LIBXML_NOENT/LIBXML_NONET flags
Entity expansion attack demonstrated through PHP parsing test

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
